spec_match.rationale: The page now names and inspects the relevant CSS surfaces: text-overflow: ellipsis, single-line overflow, focus/selection/editing interaction, RTL direction, and line-clamp as a separate multi-line boundary.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised Focus contenteditable, Select active text, Focus URL field, RTL scenario, and direction controls. The live inspector updated active element, computed style, overflow metrics, selection length, direction, and visual-check state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now delivers on the feature-index promise for input and contenteditable surfaces, and the URL-bar pattern remains as a third realistic single-line overflow case.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces RTL content, adjustable width, selection state, contenteditable selection, URL-bar fixed-prefix behavior, and the multi-line line-clamp boundary instead of only happy-path examples.

references.rationale: The page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5146265241387008 and CSS Overflow 3 ellipsis interaction, and its related links now stay under /v147/.

design_system.rationale: The rewritten UI uses shared CSS variables, sets color with custom button backgrounds, avoids raw hex colors, and fixes the previous form-field/label accessibility issue.

summary: Pre-fix DevTools MCP showed stale Chrome 148 copy/links inside a v147 page, no live readout, and form-field labeling issues. Post-fix testing in Chrome 148 at http://localhost:3000/v147/clip-text-overflow-on-user-interaction/live-edit/ exercised the contenteditable focus/selection path, URL focus, RTL scenario, direction updates, and line-clamp boundary. The page reported text-overflow CSSOM state, overflow dimensions, selected character count, direction, and manual visual-check wording. Console was clean after replacing the invalid contenteditable label with aria-labelledby. The feature conformance route at /v147/clip-text-overflow-on-user-interaction/conformance/ reported 6 PASS, 0 FAIL.
